title: Unit Converter
prompt_md:
## Unit Converter

Write a program that reads a total number of **inches** (a positive integer) from stdin and prints how many **feet** and **remaining inches** that equals.

There are 12 inches in a foot.

## Input

A single positive integer: total inches.

## Output

Two lines in exactly this format (no extra spaces or blank lines):

```
Feet: <value>
Remaining inches: <value>
```

### Example

**Input:** `37`  
**Output:**
```
Feet: 3
Remaining inches: 1
```

title: Greeting Card
prompt_md:
## Greeting Card

Write a program that reads a person's name (single word) and age (integer) from stdin, then prints a two-line greeting.

## Input

A single word (name) and an integer (age) on the same line, separated by a space.

## Output

Two lines in exactly this format (no extra blank lines):

```
Hello, <name>! You are <age> years old.
Next year you will be <age+1>.
```

### Example

**Input:** `Alice 30`  
**Output:**
```
Hello, Alice! You are 30 years old.
Next year you will be 31.
```

test_cases.label: Sample: Alice 30
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Hello, Alice! You are 30 years old.
Next year you will be 31.

test_cases.label: Hidden: Bob 1
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Hello, Bob! You are 1 years old.
Next year you will be 2.

test_cases.label: Hidden: Zara 99
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Hello, Zara! You are 99 years old.
Next year you will be 100.
